the journalist is analyzing the political dynamics of a region with three countries: A, B, and C. the influence of country A on country B is represented by a matrix $ M_{AB} $, the influence of country B on country C by $ M_{BC} $, and the influence of country C on country A by $ M_{CA} $. each matrix is a $ 2 \times 2 $ matrix with real-valued elements representing different factors of influence.  
the product of these matrices $ M_{AB} \times M_{BC} \times M_{CA} $ results in a matrix with eigenvalues $ \lambda_1 $ and $ \lambda_2 $ such that $ \lambda_1 = 3\lambda_2 $, and the trace of the product matrix is 12. find the eigenvalues $ \lambda_1 $ and $ \lambda_2 $.  
the determinant of the product matrix $ M_{AB} \times M_{BC} \times M_{CA} $ is equal to 36, find the possible values for the determinant of the matrices $ M_{AB} $, $ M_{BC} $, and $ M_{CA} $ given that they are all equal.  
the title is: "possible values for the determinant of the matrices $ M_{AB} $, $ M_{BC} $, and $ M_{CA} $ given that they are all equal."
